quizzes.title: CARSI Psychrometry & Building Science for Drying — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Dew point is the temperature at which…
options: The air becomes saturated and moisture begins to condense

questions.questionText: The actual amount of water in the air is best expressed as…
options: Specific humidity (e.g. grains per pound / g per kg)

questions.questionText: Water moves from wet materials into the air when…
options: Vapour pressure is higher in the material than in the air
The air is more humid than the material

questions.questionText: The dry standard for a material is…
options: The equilibrium moisture content of the same unaffected material
Lower the air's humidity and/or warm it to increase its capacity to hold vapour
Seal the room airtight and wait
Cool the air below dew point
Climate — Bureau of Meteorology data and NCC climate zone
